ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for iced caramel macchiato; closest parent 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301079) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent; no specific iced caramel macchiato code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34731005 'Coffee drink' is the closest parent; no specific code for iced caramel macchiato exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall (12 fl oz / 354 ml) serving of Iced Caramel Macchiato as typically served at Starbucks Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato (Tall, 2% milk) — Nutrition Information
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato (Tall, 2% milk, 354 ml). Values converted to per-100g basis. The Singapore Starbucks version is substantially identical in formulation to the US reference product. Minor rounding applied.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — milk-based sweetened coffee beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato. GI estimated at 55 based on the closest analogous food: sweetened milk-based coffee beverages (GI ~50–60) from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, AJCN). The beverage contains significant added sugars (sucrose-based vanilla syrup and caramel sauce) and dairy milk, which together produce a low to medium GI response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 36.1) / 100 ≈ 19.9, rounded to 19.5 per 354 g serving (10.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.54 = 36.1 g).

hypertension.guidance: Each tall serving provides approximately 166 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, and note that caffeine from espresso may transiently elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High added sugar and medium glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: A tall serving delivers approximately 34 g of sugar predominantly from vanilla syrup and caramel drizzle, producing a glycemic load of ~19.5; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage and consider sugar-free syrup modifications to reduce postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~3.2 g per serving from dairy) and high added sugar (~34 g per serving) may contribute to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and prefer non-fat milk or sugar-free variants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Caramel Macchiato is a cold espresso-based coffee beverage popularised globally by Starbucks. It is constructed by layering cold milk over ice, adding vanilla-flavoured syrup, marking the surface with shots of espresso poured through the foam, and finishing with a crosshatch caramel sauce drizzle on top. The word 'macchiato' means 'marked' in Italian, referring to the espresso mark on the milk. The drink delivers a sweet, creamy, and mildly bitter flavour profile with a smooth, cold texture. In Singapore, it is sold at Starbucks outlets including One Fullerton and is priced at approximately SGD 8.50 for a tall size. Nutritionally, a tall (354 ml) serving provides approximately 220 kcal, 8.9 g protein, 5.0 g fat, and 36 g carbohydrate, with the majority of carbohydrates derived from added sugars in the vanilla syrup and caramel drizzle. The beverage has a low glycemic index (~55) and a glycemic load of approximately 19.5 per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It contains dairy milk and is therefore not suitable for lactose-intolerant or vegan consumers. The caffeine content from espresso shots provides a stimulant effect. Clinically, the high sugar content warrants caution for diabetic patients, and the sodium and caffeine content are relevant for hypertensive individuals.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an iced caramel macchiato?
Can diabetics drink iced caramel macchiato?
Iced caramel macchiato GI glycemic index
What is in an iced caramel macchiato?
Iced caramel macchiato vs iced latte

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato (Tall, 2% milk), which is the closest approved-source match. The Singapore Starbucks formulation is substantially identical to the US reference product. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for sweetened milk-based coffee beverages, as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the approved literature.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the beverage as served (liquid weight basis). Serving size of 354 g corresponds to a Starbucks tall (12 fl oz) cup. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero; this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; coffee beverages of this type contain no meaningful vitamin C. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 7. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 9. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 10. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. 11. GI is an analogous estimate (~55, low) based on sweetened milk-based coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Starbucks Iced Caramel Macchiato exists in approved sources. 12. Halal status reflects standard Starbucks Singapore certification; kosher status not confirmed. 13. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Singapore-served food items. 14. The cuisine category is Coffee/Western; Singapore multilingual naming rules applied as the food is served in Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch using USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods as nutrient source. Applied Singapore multilingual naming rules (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Set GI to 55 (medium) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk-based coffee beverages. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per GI classification rules (GI <= 55 is low). Updated relevant notes and reviewer summary to reflect this correction.
